commentaries.author: Ambrosiaster
commentaries.content: The imitator and lover of the saints will practice hospitality following the examples of Abraham and Lot as righteous men. Commentary on Paul’s Epistles.

commentaries.author: Cyprian of Carthage
commentaries.content: Distributing to the necessity of saints; given to hospitality. Cyprian of Carthage Or our work will He say now, "I was captive, and ye redeemed me. "And since again He says, "I was in prison, and ye came unto me "how much more will it be when He begins to say, "I was in the dungeon of captivity, and I lay shut up and bound among barbarians, and from that prison of slavery you delivered me "being about to receive a reward from the Lord when the day of judgment shall come. Finally, we give you the warmest thanks that you have wished us to be sharers in your anxiety,.

commentaries.author: Diodorus of Tarsus
commentaries.content: The point here is that we should honor the saints and take care of their needs until they no longer have them. .

commentaries.author: Gennadius of Constantinople
commentaries.content: Paul calls the believers “saints” because they have been called to be holy. .

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Communicating to the necessities of the saints. Making them partakers of what you have, by relieving them. (Witham)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
